mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production signals stronger economic output and rising corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO with 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive returns following higher INDPRO readings is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher unemployment rate indicates labor market weakness and lower consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The inverse correlation between UNRATE and forward 5-day returns fails to be negative in at least 70% of subsamples, or the mean negative return gap is less than -0.1% when UNRATE is above the median.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a threshold of 2.0%, it signals aggressive tightening that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In events when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in payroll employment and rising policy rates creates stagflationary signals: cooling labor demand reduces earnings prospects while higher rates compress valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month declines and FEDFUNDS month-over-month increases
failure_condition: The average 20-day return after both conditions are met is not lower than the unconditional average by at least 1% (one-tailed t-test p < 0.05), or the signal generates fewer than 30 distinct events over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising housing starts signal that the housing sector remains robust despite higher rates, indicating underlying demand strength and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods identified as tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS increasing over last 6 months), the rank IC of HOUST with 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or the mean return on HOUST above median is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.05), or the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 for more than half the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the threshold of 290 (level), inflation is sufficiently high to erode real earnings and trigger tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 290, the average 20-day forward return is not negative enough to be distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10), or fewer than 40 trigger events occur in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Combined rise in industrial production and decline in unemployment signals a robust expansion phase where economic growth is self-reinforcing, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 105 and UNRATE falls below 3.5
failure_condition: The average 5-day return when both conditions are satisfied is not significantly above the unconditional average (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's win rate (positive returns) is below 60%.
